scenarios.title: Task-light restraint on familiar work
scenarios.prompt: Use $self-evolving-agent for this situation: I only need to update one familiar README sentence. Classify the task, keep it in `task_light`, retrieve only the minimum context, and give me one verification check.

criteria.name: mode selection discipline
criteria.description: Keeps the task in task_light mode or an equivalent smallest-safe framing.

criteria.name: selective retrieval
criteria.description: Retrieves only a minimal relevant context set rather than expanding into task_full.

criteria.name: verification check
criteria.description: Provides a small but explicit verification step before delivery.

criteria.name: avoids task-full bloat
criteria.description: Does not produce unnecessary training, agenda, or promotion output for a trivial task.

scenarios.title: Pre-task capability risk diagnosis
scenarios.prompt: Use $self-evolving-agent for this situation: I need to modify a production deployment workflow I have never touched before. Before doing any edits, classify the task, retrieve targeted context, give me a pre-task capability risk diagnosis, and propose an execution strategy with a verification plan.

criteria.name: mode classification
criteria.description: Classifies the task into a heavier mode before execution.

criteria.name: retrieval plan
criteria.description: Identifies which prior records should be retrieved and why.

criteria.name: capability risk diagnosis
criteria.description: Names likely weak capabilities or failure modes relevant to the task.

criteria.name: verification-first strategy
criteria.description: Proposes an execution strategy and explicit verification plan before edits.

scenarios.title: Post-task diagnosis and training-unit generation
scenarios.prompt: Use $self-evolving-agent after this incident: I edited a release command by guessing a flag name, the user corrected me, and I now realize I skipped validating the tool contract. Produce the post-task diagnosis, decide whether the task should stay in `task_light` or escalate to `task_full`, update the capability evidence, and create a training unit if warranted.

criteria.name: capability-level diagnosis
criteria.description: Frames the issue as a capability weakness, not only as a surface incident.

criteria.name: escalation judgment
criteria.description: Explains whether the situation should escalate beyond task_light mode and why.

criteria.name: evidence update
criteria.description: Updates capability evidence or next-focus logic using the incident.

criteria.name: training restraint
criteria.description: Creates a training unit only when the pattern is structural or recurring.

scenarios.title: Missed retrieval recovery
scenarios.prompt: Use $self-evolving-agent for this follow-up: I repeated a mistake because I failed to retrieve a relevant prior lesson in time. Reclassify the situation, show which records should have been retrieved, and explain how the retrieval strategy should change.

criteria.name: retrieval-failure diagnosis
criteria.description: Identifies memory retrieval as the operative weakness.

criteria.name: missed-record surfacing
criteria.description: Names the kind of records that should have been pulled in before action.

criteria.name: strategy update
criteria.description: Proposes a trigger-signature or retrieval change that would prevent the repeat.

criteria.name: no vague recap
criteria.description: Goes beyond generic reflection and gives a concrete retrieval correction.

scenarios.title: Evaluation state and promotion decision
scenarios.prompt: Use $self-evolving-agent for this follow-up: a verification-first strategy previously trained on deployment commands later helped me correctly update a different release automation task without user correction. Decide the evaluation state and whether the strategy should be promoted.

criteria.name: evaluation ladder discipline
criteria.description: Places the learning at the correct state on the recorded-to-promoted ladder.

criteria.name: transfer evidence
criteria.description: Uses the new scenario as transfer evidence rather than treating the original recording as enough.

criteria.name: promotion gate
criteria.description: Makes a scoped promotion decision with trigger signature and minimal durable rule.

criteria.name: overgeneralization control
criteria.description: Acknowledges limits or risks instead of promoting a broad vague rule.

scenarios.title: Learning agenda review
scenarios.prompt: Use $self-evolving-agent before the next unfamiliar project: I have completed five meaningful task cycles, verification is improving, but I still miss relevant past learnings and sometimes skip my intended workflow under pressure. Run a learning agenda review and decide the top 1-3 capabilities to train next.

criteria.name: agenda trigger recognition
criteria.description: Recognizes that the scenario should trigger a learning agenda review.

criteria.name: small active focus set
criteria.description: Chooses only 1-3 active focus capabilities instead of an unbounded list.

criteria.name: prioritization rationale
criteria.description: Explains why these capabilities matter now relative to alternatives.

criteria.name: exit criteria
criteria.description: Defines what evidence would retire or advance each active focus.
